steps.title: Identify the shared problem in scholarship
steps.content:
Both texts show that scholarship has a distortingly narrow focus.

Text 1 demonstrates how overemphasis on fragmented narrative obscures the actual influence of different narrative styles on modernist fiction.

Text 2 demonstrates how fixation on the fragmentation versus coherence distinction inhibits inquiry into other shared stylistic patterns.

steps.title: Match this problem to the statement about its consequence
steps.content:
A directly names what both texts illustrate: without a major shift in focus, scholarship's vision of the modernist period will remain unnecessarily limited.
  • B mischaracterizes what needs to change
  • C invents an unsupported claim about obsolescence
  • D emphasizes rehabilitating works rather than addressing the core problem
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
